metadata.summary: Libhum is a Python GPU-accelerated library to extract and compare Electricity Frequency Signals (ENF)

documentation:
# Libhum

![Libhum computing an ENF signal](images/compute_enf.png)

Libhum is a Python library and set of utilities to extract and compare
[electricity frequency signals](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electrical_network_frequency_analysis)
(ENF).

The library uses GPU accelaration (CUDA or OpenCL) to accurately match ENF signals in seconds.

On an Apple Macbook Air M1, the library is able to match an 1 hour signal over a 10 years history in
about 20 seconds.

# Setup

```
pip install libhum
```

# Compute ENF

Use the `compute_enf` command to extract the main ENF from an audio or video file to an `.enf` file:

```
libhum compute_enf samples/target.ogg target.enf --plot
```

By default, the library is looking for an ENF signal in the 50 Hz band. Use the `-f/--frequency`
option to use a different frequency.

# Match ENFs

![Libhum matching two ENF signals](images/match_enf.png)

Use the `match_enf` command to find the best matches between two signals:

```
libhum match_enf samples/reference.enf samples/target.enf --plot
```

The default backend is using Numpy and is considerably slower than the OpenCL or CUDA backends which
rely on GPU accelaration. Append the `--backend=cuda` or `--backend=opencl` option to use a
GPU-accelerated backend.

# Special thanks

Some of the signal processing algorithms used by the library originate from Robert Heaton's
[ENF matching library](https://github.com/robert/enf-matching).

# License

See [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

# BibTeX Citation

If you use Libhum in any scientific publication, please use the following BibTex citation:

```
@software{Javaux_Libhum,
    author = {Javaux, Raphael},
    license = {LGPL-3.0},
    title = {{Libhum}},
    url = {https://github.com/RaphaelJ/libhum},
    year={2024},
}
```

functions.name: libhum.merge.merge_signals
documentation:
Combines the two ENF signal in a single continuous signal, based on their respective
time stamps.

classes.name: libhum.merge.MergeStrategy
documentation: How the merge algorithm handles overlapping values.

functions.name: libhum.io.read_signal
documentation: Reads an ENF signal from a file.

functions.name: libhum.io.write_signal
documentation: Writes an ENF signal to a file.

functions.name: libhum.io.read_audio
documentation: Reads an audio file and returns its single channel buffer with its sampling frequency.

functions.name: libhum.io.read_weti
documentation:
Reads a reference ENF signal from a CSV file produced by the Wind Energy Technology Institute.

See https://osf.io/jbk82/.

functions.name: libhum.io.fetch_swiss_grid
documentation:
Fetches the latest 10 minute data from the Swiss Grid operator.

See https://www.swissgrid.ch/en/home/operation/grid-data/current-data.html

functions.name: libhum.io.fetch_uk_grid
documentation:
Fetches one month of data from the UK National Grid operator.

See https://data.nationalgrideso.com/system/system-frequency-data

functions.name: libhum.io.fetch_swedish_grid
documentation:
Fetches up to one hour of data from the Swedish national grid operator.

See https://www.svk.se/en/national-grid/the-control-room/

functions.name: libhum.io._resample_sparse_signal
documentation:
Samples the sparse signal at the requested frequency.

Returns the resampled signal, and the begin time.

classes.name: libhum.types.Signal

class_variables.name: libhum.types.Signal.network_frequency
documentation: e.g. 50Hz or 60Hz

class_variables.name: libhum.types.Signal.signal_frequency
documentation: e.g. 1Hz or 0.1Hz
An ENF signal with its associated attributes.

classes.name: libhum.types.Match
documentation: A single result of a time matching comparison between two ENF signals.

classes.name: libhum.types.AnalysisResult
documentation: The result of the detection of an ENF signal from an audio file.

modules.name: libhum.analysis
documentation: Provides DSP algorithms to extract ENF signal from sound recordings.
Copyright (C) 2023 Raphael Javaux
raphaeljavaux@gmail.com
This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or
modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public
License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either
version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later version.

Lesser General Public License for more details.
You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public License
along with this program; if not, write to the Free Software Foundation,
Inc., 51 Franklin Street, Fifth Floor, Boston, MA  02110-1301, USA.
Some of the filtering functions have been heavily inspired by Robert Heaton's implementation of
ENF analysis:
https://github.com/robert/enf-matching

functions.name: libhum.analysis.compute_enf
documentation:
Detects the ENF signal in the provided audio signal.

Returns None if no ENF signal could be detected.

functions.name: libhum.analysis._signal_decimate
documentation:
Decimates the input signal to at least `MIN_DECIMATED_FREQUENCY`.

Returns the new signal frequency and the decimated signal.

functions.name: libhum.analysis._signal_spectrums
documentation:
Computes the normalized STFT spectrums for the given network frequency's harmonics.

Returns the frequencies, timestamp, and spectrum for every requested harmonic's frequency band.

functions.name: libhum.analysis._bandpass_filter
documentation:
Passes input data through a Butterworth bandpass filter. Code borrowed from
https://scipy-cookbook.readthedocs.io/items/ButterworthBandpass.html

functions.name: libhum.analysis._stft
documentation:
Performs a Short-time Fourier Transform (STFT) on the input signal for the given bands

Return the frequencies, timestamps and spectrum for each requested frequency band.

functions.name: libhum.analysis._spectrum_normalize
documentation: Normalizes to the mean and stddev over NORMALIZE_WINDOW_SIZE.

functions.name: libhum.analysis._detect_enf
documentation: Detects the ENF signal at ENF_OUTPUT_FREQUENCY in the normalized spectrum.

functions.name: libhum.analysis._quadratic_interpolation
documentation: https://ccrma.stanford.edu/~jos/sasp/Quadratic_Interpolation_Spectral_Peaks.html

functions.name: libhum.analysis._threshold_enf
libhum.analysis._combined_harmonics_result
documentation: Re-runs the ENF detection on the combined spectrum of the provided ENF analysis results.

functions.name: libhum.analysis._combine_spectrums
documentation:
Adds the source spectrum to the target spectrum, with their associated weights.

Returns a spectrum object that match the target object's shape.

variables.name: libhum.analysis.SPECTRUM_BAND_SIZE
documentation: e.g. 49.75 to 50.25 for 50Hz ENF.

variables.name: libhum.analysis.ENF_OUTPUT_FREQUENCY
documentation: Detects the source ENF at 1Hz

modules.name: libhum.match
documentation: Copyright (C) 2023 Raphael Javaux
raphaeljavaux@gmail.com
This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or

functions.name: libhum.match._compute_filtered_corr_coeffs
documentation:
Computes the correlation coefficients for all the requested offsets using the requested
backend instance.

functions.name: libhum.match._compute_corr_coeffs_numpy
documentation: Computes the Pearson's correlation coefficients for the requested offsets.

functions.name: libhum.match._corr_coeff
documentation:
Computes the Pearson's correlation coefficient of two masked arrays.

Ignore the masked samples and returns the total number of non-masked samples in the two signals.

functions.name: libhum.match._compute_rmses
documentation: Compute the Root Mean Square Errors for the requested offsets

functions.name: libhum.match._rmse
documentation:
Computes the Root Mean Square Error of two masked arrays.

Ignore the masked samples.

functions.name: libhum.match._filter_coeffs
documentation: Filters out bad matches based on their coefficients.

functions.name: libhum.match._build_matches
documentation:
Post-processes the matches's coefficients by filtering poor matches and by merging adjacent
matches.

Computes additional features and builds `Match` objects.

functions.name: libhum.match._match_score_linear_func
documentation: Linear function used by the serialized linear regression estimator in `_match_scores()`.

functions.name: libhum.match._match_scores
documentation: Estimates a probabilistic score ([0..1]) of a match.

variables.name: libhum.match.MAX_BUFFER_SIZE
documentation: 64 MB

variables.name: libhum.match.THREADS_PER_BLOCK
documentation: CUDA threads per bloc or OpenCL workers per group
